title: swida sanguinea subsp. australis
intro: Swida sanguinea subsp. australis, also known as the New Zealand bloodberry, is a deciduous shrub native to New Zealand and a subspecies of the European guelder rose. The medicinal properties of Swida sanguinea subsp. australis lie in its berries, which are rich in anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits associated with consuming the berries include reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and potential anti-cancer properties. Swida sanguinea subsp. australis is typically harvested for its berries, which can be made into teas, infusions, and jams, or used in traditional herbal preparations.
